text: Sign Michael up for swim lessons
Buy garden bed supplies and lay cardboard
notes: Day 1 of garden bed project in backyard near walkout
subtasks: Lay cardboard on 4x8 area with 6-inch overlaps

text: Mix amendments and layer on garden bed
notes: Days 2-3 of garden bed project. Blood meal needs to mellow to avoid burning roots
subtasks: Mix blood meal and bone meal into compost/topsoil
Layer 4-6 inches of mixture on wet cardboard

text: Check soil temperature for planting readiness
notes: Don't plant until soil consistently reads 65°F. This is days 10-14 of project
subtasks: Use thermometer to check soil temp
Verify soil has been undisturbed for 5-7 days minimum

text: Plant garden bed crops
notes: Planting day (day 10-14). Tomatoes root along buried stem. Handle cucumber roots gently. Peppers like heat and benefit from tomato shade later
subtasks: Plant cucumbers on north end with trellis
Plant 2 tomatoes in center with cages (bury deep to first leaves)
Plant 2-3 peppers on south end (same depth as pot)
Water in with diluted fish emulsion immediately

text: Establish watering routine and mulch plants
notes: Weeks 3-4. These crops hate wet/dry cycles. Let plants establish before fertilizing
subtasks: Water consistently 1 inch per week minimum
Mulch with straw or wood chips 3-5 days after planting

text: Side-dress plants when flowering begins
notes: Week 6 when flowers appear. Avoid high nitrogen which creates huge plants with little fruit
subtasks: Apply blood meal lightly around each plant
Switch to low-nitrogen, higher phosphorus fertilizer (like 5-10-5)
Focus on fruit set rather than leaf growth

text: Ongoing summer garden maintenance
notes: Ongoing through summer. Don't leave overripe cucumbers on vine as it stops production
subtasks: Pinch tomato suckers weekly, keep 1-2 main stems
Harvest cucumbers aggressively to keep plants producing
Leave peppers largely alone (they don't like fussing)

text: Establish watering routine and mulch plants
notes: Weeks 3-4. Don't fertilize again yet during establishment period.
subtasks: Water consistently 1 inch per week minimum
Mulch around plants with straw or wood chips
Wait 3-5 days after planting before mulching

text: Switch to flowering fertilizer when flowers appear
notes: Week 6. Most gardeners make mistake of continuing nitrogen feeding.
subtasks: Side-dress lightly with blood meal around each plant
Switch to low-nitrogen, higher phosphorus liquid fertilizer (5-10-5 ratio)
Focus on fruit set rather than leaf growth

text: Begin ongoing summer garden maintenance
notes: Ongoing through summer. Year one is experimental - bed will be better in year two.
subtasks: Pinch tomato suckers weekly, keep to 1-2 main stems
Harvest cucumbers aggressively to keep production going
Leave peppers alone - they don't like fussing
Monitor for drainage issues and weed pressure from edges
